with open(qpath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    q = json.load(f)
with open(hpath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    h = json.load(f)

    elif 'Matn' in h[i]:

# define normalization functions for Arabic
import re, unicodedata
arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED]')
tatweel = '\u0640'
def normalize_ar(text):
    # Replace tatweel
    text = text.replace(tatweel, '')
    # Normalize hamza forms: convert all hamza variants to bare hamza? We'll map common variants to أ or ا? 
    # Simplify by mapping: أ إ آ ء to ا? But instructions said unify hamza; let's map all hamza-chars to ا
    text = re.sub(r'[أإآء]', 'ا', text)
    # remove diacritics
    text = arabic_diacritics.sub('', text)
    # remove punctuation and extra symbols
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', text)  # keep Arabic letters and digits and underscore? \w includes _ and digits and Latin; but fine
    # remove Latin letters/digits underscores etc.
    text = re.sub(r'[_\dA-Za-z]', '', text)
    # collapse spaces and lowercase (Arabic doesn't have case)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

# Test normalize on target string
target = "قَالَ ٱللَّهُ إِنِّي سَأَلۡتُمۡ مِن قَبۡلُۢ فَمَنۡ فَرَٰضَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَكُورٌۭ حَلِيمٌ"
print("orig:", target)
print("norm:", normalize_ar(target))

# Search q list for entries containing words like شكور حليم
norm_target = normalize_ar(target)
candidates = []
for entry in q:
    ay = entry.get('ayah_text','')
    if 'شَكُور' in ay or 'شَكُورٌ' in ay or 'شَكُورٌۭ' in ay or 'شکور' in ay or 'شَكُورٌ' in ay:
        candidates.append(entry)
len(candidates)

for e in candidates:
    print("surah_id, ayah_id:", e.get('surah_id'), e.get('ayah_id'))
    print(e.get('ayah_text'))
    print("---")

# find entries with surah_name 'الحاقة' or surah_id 69
haq_entries = [e for e in q if e.get('surah_name')=='الحاقة' or e.get('surah_id')==69]
len(haq_entries)

from difflib import SequenceMatcher
def norm_for_match(s):
    # Normalize as per normalize_ar but produce lower-case etc.
    return normalize_ar(s)
full_span = "قَالَ ٱللَّهُ إِنِّي سَأَلۡتُمۡ مِن قَبۡلُۢ فَمَنۡ فَرَٰضَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَكُورٌۭ حَلِيمٌ"
norm_full = norm_for_match(full_span)

# compute similarity ratio for each ayah
best = []
for entry in q:
    ay = entry.get('ayah_text','')
    ratio = SequenceMatcher(None, norm_full, normalize_ar(ay)).ratio()
    best.append((ratio, entry))
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
for r, e in best_sorted[:20]:
    print(r, e.get('surah_id'), e.get('ayah_id'), e.get('ayah_text'))
